notes: TTS Studio version 2.1.8: Added real-time playback and drag-seeking synchronization between the active segment card's progress bar/play buttons and the main combined audio player., Unified user segment card selection with the active playhead segment: the selected segment card now automatically follows the combined player's playhead position during active playbacks and seeks, maintaining a single, consistent selection indicator across the interface., Added hover-based auto-scroll controls to the transcript preview: auto-scrolling the active segment card into view is paused while the user's cursor is inside the Transcript Preview panel, preventing layout scroll fights, and automatically catches up when the cursor leaves the panel., Fixed a thread lock issue that caused the application to freeze or lag when the transcription queue finished processing files by cleaning resolved promises from the active queue array., Fixed audio segment playback in Scriber by implementing a standard HTML5 pause-seek-play state machine, ensuring precise segment seeking without audio bleeding from previous sections., Fixed segment playback in "One Segment / Clip: Off" mode by propagating and merging the precise `endSec` float boundary when transcript segments are stitched together, preventing premature audio cutoffs on merged cards., Fixed a layout scrolling performance lag in the transcript preview list by throttling `.scrollIntoView()` triggers to run only when the active segment card index actually changes, rather than firing continuously on every `currentTime` frame update, eliminating browser layout engine thrashing., Fixed segment loops to be gapless and seamless., Fixed a bug where generated Speech Audio cache files could be deleted on application startup if generation was interrupted or stopped mid-flight., Separated segment-level playback and loop controls from the main playlist player. Segment playbacks now run on a dedicated background audio channel, leaving the main player's timeline, progress bar, and playhead position completely unaffected., Added a raw, clean, long horizontal progress slider to the active segment card's header row, showing both the elapsed time and the total segment duration (e.g., 1.2s / 2.5s) next to the bar. Optimized the segment player state machine to keep the audio source cached when paused, avoiding stutters or loading pauses during click play/pause and rendering instant, fluid playbacks. Fixed last-word audio clipping by introducing a 400ms natural tail decay buffer (capped at file duration) and preventing early audio stream teardowns when playbacks finish, allowing full and smooth segment rendering. Decoupled drag and seek states to make the visual slider thumb drag behavior 100% fluid, responsive, and lag-free. Disabled forced autoplay when selecting/clicking transcript segments, allowing users to select cards and seek the main player position without triggering playback automatically, and prevented event bubbling during segment seeker drags. Added global keyboard hotkeys: "Space" to play/pause the currently selected segment (safely disabled when editing text/inputs), and "ArrowUp" / "ArrowDown" keys to navigate and select the previous/next transcript segment card, automatically scrolling selected segments into view and updating the main player timeline position. Decoupled segment selection from active playback loading states, fixing a focus issue where selecting a new segment and pressing Spacebar would still play/pause the previously active segment. Resolved a visual playball skipping bug on play trigger by launching progress tracking synchronously alongside the play request, bypassing the asynchronous browser audio promise resolution latency. Prevented seek-latency jumps in segments located at non-zero offsets (segments 2+) by implementing a reliable HTML5 wait chain (waiting for "canplay" then "seeked" events before starting audio play and animation ticks), and clamped progress indicators to the exact segment duration boundaries., Fixed a visual lag and skipping issue on the segment player's progress bar (playball) by launching the progress tracking timer immediately on all playback events (including starting different segments, looping, and finishing drag gestures) rather than waiting for background audio engine promises., Refactored the Segment Player to use declarative JSX DOM audio listeners and a hybrid high-resolution playback clock using performance.now() synced with the native onPlaying event. This bypasses WebKit throttling on audio currentTime updates, resolving starting visual jumps (such as 0s to 0.7s) and preventing event listener crosstalk when switching between segments. Optimized playback transitions for segments within the same file to seek directly without reloading the media file, eliminated playback resume race conditions by clearing pending callbacks on pause, and synced native HTML5 onEnded callbacks for secure looping/completion boundaries., Fixed a visual playball jump and snapping issue on segment player playback (especially on Segment 2+) by injecting precise, raw float values (`startSec` and `endSec`) to each segment. This decouples user-friendly string time formats (which drop sub-seconds) from underlying audio seeking and boundary calculations, eliminating all rounding distortions.
